stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM in the current AI landscape.
It is presented via Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating community interest and performance tracking.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation,' confirming its primary application as a generative AI model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of NLP/Generative AI, specifically using the 'text-generation' pipeline.
The model is associated with key technologies like transformers and safetensors, indicating its technical relevance.

stories.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: NASA has selected Relativity Space for a 2028 mission to Mars.
The mission involves launching NASA's Aeolus payload to Mars.
Relativity Space will handle the spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations under a public-private partnership.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has identified a new malware, 'Crypto Clipper,' capable of stealing cryptocurrency.
The malware utilizes a lightweight backdoor mechanism and spreads via USB drives.
Communication for the malware is routed through the Tor network, complicating detection.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: Researchers found a causally actionable activation-space direction to detect misalignment in language models.
Misalignment emerges when models are fine-tuned on insecure code, affecting internal structure.
This detection method achieved 99.6% separation across four different instruction-tuned model families.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical LLM deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
Associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, pointing to modern NLP infrastructure.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model outputs.
The updates also result in faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration enables faster performance when running Ollama specifically on Apple Silicon devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects users to local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI functionality while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: The Atlantic has made public four datasets of music used to train AI models.
Two of the discovered music datasets are massive, containing 12 million and 9 million tracks respectively.
This development highlights the transparency and scale of data fueling modern AI training.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: The FDA advisors unanimously voted to approve Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of 'agency drama,' notably involving a Trump official refusing review in February.
This signifies a major regulatory milestone for mRNA technology in vaccine development.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production for national defense.
The increased drone spending could have positive economic spillover effects overseas.
The context of looming geopolitical tensions with China is a key driver for this defense buildup.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses a key limitation in state-of-the-art flow-matching TTS: the inability to adapt to new pronunciations post-deployment.
It introduces a lifelong adaptation framework that learns pronunciation corrections via latent conditioning edits rather than full model retraining.
The method optimizes token-level perturbations in the text embedding space when corrective feedback is provided, allowing for fine-grained pronunciation tuning.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces 'StylisticBias,' a new benchmark to evaluate attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in MLLM research by separating appearance-based bias from inherent identity differences.
Focuses on how subtle visual cues influence how MLLMs judge people, which is crucial given their deployment in consequential settings.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: The paper proposes a novel LLM alignment method that utilizes implicit user feedback instead of relying solely on expensive explicit human feedback.
Existing alignment methods are limited by the difficulty and cost of collecting high-quality explicit preference annotations from users.
This approach aims to leverage implicit signals, similar to those used by major internet platforms, to improve LLM alignment efficiency.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across diverse hardware.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Hue’s wired wall modules bring non-smart lights into its ecosystem
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Philips Hue introduced wired wall modules to integrate non-smart lights into its ecosystem.
The new modules are designed to be installed behind existing wall switches.
Hue also released more affordable versions of its Signe series lamps and updated E14 bulbs.

stories.title: The UK will scan asylum-seekers’ faces for age checks—despite knowing the tech is flawed
stories.author: Matt Burgess, Maddy Varner, May Bulman, Gabriel Geiger, WIRED.com
bullet_points: The UK plans to implement facial scanning technology for age verification of asylum seekers.
Independent tests have demonstrated significant risks of life-altering errors with the technology.
The deployment proceeds despite known flaws in the age-verification system.

stories.title: NASA asks Northrop Grumman to stop working on lunar HALO module
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has directed Northrop Grumman to cease work on the lunar HALO module.
The decision involves reassigning the majority of the affected employees to other ongoing programs.
This signals a potential shift or change in NASA's lunar exploration hardware strategy.

stories.title: Android verification is coming: Google confirms timeline and supported app stores
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is implementing a new Android verification system service this month.
Significant changes related to this verification are scheduled to begin in September.
The update confirms timelines and details regarding supported app stores.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM that operates significantly in a continuous latent space.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of clarity regarding how individual words influence the acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
It proposes a novel application of cross-attention attribution, adapting the DAAM framework to speech diffusion models for the first time.
The method generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers to improve the diagnosis of failure modes and controllability in expressive TTS.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for AI agents.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where state information (facts, constraints) must be reconstructed from the prompt each turn.
Focuses on improving policy-adherent tool-calling agents, particularly relevant for complex customer-service applications.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: DeepSWIP addresses a limitation in neurosymbolic systems (like DeepProbLog) by introducing a single-world counterfactual semantics.
The system integrates neural perception with probabilistic logic programming, moving beyond standard associational inference.
It achieves counterfactual reasoning by using neural materialization to map neural predicates to ProbLog choices and applying Single World Intervention Programs (SWIPs).

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: SARLO-80 introduces a new, large-scale dataset for combining Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) and optical imagery.
The dataset addresses a critical gap by providing high-resolution, physically grounded SAR data, unlike existing intensity-only benchmarks.
This resource is crucial for advancing multimodal foundation models in remote sensing applications.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of LiveCodeBench (LCB) to support multiple programming languages.
LCB is a recognized benchmark for evaluating LLMs on code generation, offering contamination-aware evaluation.
The research addresses a key limitation of LCB, which was previously restricted only to Python.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as an alternative to traditional Differential Privacy (DP).
This new framework offers a fine-grained privacy measure that explicitly accounts for attacker knowledge and query types.
It aims to address the costly worst-case privacy-accuracy tradeoff inherent in standard DP.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in LLM serving by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios, contrasting with high-throughput KV cache methods.
Introduces 'Execution-State Capsules' for checkpointing and restoring the full execution state, not just the KV cache.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require frequent state resets and tight responsiveness.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation of LLM coding agents: their lack of high-level operational knowledge about a software repository.
The paper investigates the effectiveness of using external context files (like AGENTS.md) to guide coding agents.
It proposes a 'Probe-and-Refine' tuning method to improve agent performance by incorporating repository guidance.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to give policy-adherent agents a structured task state.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where task state (facts, constraints, conditions) must be reconstructed from the prompt each turn.
Focuses on improving reliability in complex domains like customer service by explicitly managing state across tool calls and policy adherence.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale, bilingual (German/English) dataset of 1.2M CT/MR image-text pairs for radiology.
Develops RefRad2D to enable training of spatially grounded Vision-Language Models (VLMs) without manual spatial annotations.
The resulting model, RadGrounder, performs joint tasks including report generation and visual question answering in a medical context.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between hate speech and misinformation, a gap in current NLP research.
Proposes a new dataset ('CATCH-ME') to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for generating counterspeech.
Aims to improve LLM performance in generating non-repetitive and relevant responses against harmful online exchanges.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper focuses on token-oriented inference optimization, a critical area for making large models scalable and cost-effective.
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture: Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.
The research provides a systematic review of current key technologies and industry status across these optimization levels.

stories.title: PsyScore: A Psychometrically-Aware Framework for Trait-Adaptive Essay Scoring and ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback
stories.author: Chanjin Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces PsyScore, a novel framework designed to integrate Automated Essay Scoring (AES) with instructional feedback.
Addresses limitations in current AES by making the scoring process 'psychometrically-aware' to account for learner proficiency.
Aims to move beyond fragmented systems where scoring and feedback are treated separately, enhancing both assessment reliability and feedback actionability.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF), a nine-dimension schema for analyzing Nigerian public discourse.
Argues that current sentiment analysis benchmarks (like NaijaSenti) are too simplistic, treating discourse as only positive/negative/neutral.
Identifies 'context failure' rather than translation failure as the primary obstacle for AI systems understanding Nigerian language nuances.

stories.title: AWS WAF Brought Back 402 Payment Required for the Agent Economy
stories.author: ikenyal
bullet_points: AWS WAF now includes a feature called 'AI traffic monetization'.
This feature allows website owners to charge AI bots that interact with their sites.
It represents a new mechanism for monetizing traffic generated by automated AI agents.
